chain: Identify the study goal as disease association (case/control or phenotype-related), not community description alone.
Host-associated gut samples need host depletion before taxonomic and functional profiling.
Prefer shotgun metagenomics: covers species plus pathways/AMR in one assay.
For high-complexity gut communities, prefer MEGAHIT for assembly; differential analysis requires group metadata.
Interpretation must be grounded in GTDB/NCBI and literature; avoid invented taxa and overstated causality.

citations.note: Community best-practice MAG / metagenome workflow patterns
Community Q&A on tool choice and contamination

chain: Goal: differential abundance plus mechanistic hypotheses; requires statistics + literature/evidence.
Focus on testable hypotheses such as Faecalibacterium depletion and Escherichia enrichment.
Functional layer (KEGG/eggNOG) for SCFA/inflammation-related pathways; not for clinical diagnosis.
All taxon claims must be GTDB/NCBI-anchored with p/q values and PMIDs.

citations.note: When amplicon is chosen instead of shotgun
Community discussion on DA tool caveats

chain: Soil is highly complex with diverse hosts; viral signal is often low-abundance and needs dedicated virus tools or enrichment.
Taxonomy may use ViWrap/PhaBOX; do not assert viral diversity from bacteria-oriented databases alone.
Assembly may use MEGAHIT; viral contigs need extra QC to avoid treating bacterial fragments as viruses.
Reasoning must cite viral metagenomics best practices; never invent virus species names.

citations.note: Prefer community pipelines for complex environmental data
Tool selection for virome / low-abundance viruses

chain: Clarify genome-recovery goal: QC → assembly → binning → CheckM2 → GTDB-Tk.
Use MEGAHIT for high complexity; try metaSPAdes for low-complexity / small communities.
Peak resource use is high; production runs should use Nextflow/Snakemake -resume.
